summary: Small matching fix in src/sysdolphin/baselib/robj.c for expLoadDesc. The PR swapped the conditional assignments for exp->expr.func so that non-NULL desc->func is copied into exp->expr.func, while a NULL desc->func falls back to dummy_func. This corrected the decompilation match for the robj expression descriptor loader.

what_changed.details: Changed the desc->func handling branch: when desc->func is non-NULL, exp->expr.func is now assigned desc->func instead of dummy_func; when desc->func is NULL, exp->expr.func is now assigned dummy_func instead of NULL.
No broader structural changes were made; the PR was a two-line assignment swap inside the existing desc != NULL and desc->func != NULL checks.

smart_moves: The fix kept the existing control-flow structure and only swapped the assigned values, which is a low-risk way to correct a decompilation mismatch when branch shape is already right but per-branch values are reversed.
The change preserves surrounding initialization behavior: memset(exp, 0, sizeof(HSD_Exp)), loadRvalue(desc->rvalue), and exp->nb_args = -1 are left untouched.
decomp_lessons: For matching fixes, inspect whether a mismatch is caused by branch payloads rather than branch structure; a simple assignment swap can be enough to recover the intended codegen.

When decompiling loader functions like expLoadDesc, NULL/default fallback behavior may be counterintuitive. Verify both the non-NULL source pointer path and the fallback path against the target assembly before assuming dummy functions are only used for present descriptors.
Small semantic-looking fixes can be matching-driven; this PR title explicitly frames the change as fixing the expLoadDesc match in robj.
naming_conventions: Existing naming uses HSD_Exp and HSD_ExpDesc types for expression data and descriptors.
The loader helper is named expLoadDesc, following a compact lower-camel/internal helper style.
The fallback callable is named dummy_func, using snake_case for a local/static dummy function symbol.
The rvalue loader helper is named loadRvalue, using lower-camel style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Retain the matched control-flow skeleton and adjust only the branch assignment targets when the emitted code is close but reversed.
For conditional pointer assignments, test the generated code for both desc->func != NULL and desc->func == NULL cases; the branch ordering may match while the selected values do not.
Use minimal diffs for matching repairs so later reviewers can isolate exactly which expression affected codegen.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
There was one issue comment counted in the PR metadata, but its body was not included in the provided excerpt, so no evidence-grounded feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching expLoadDesc or dummy_func in src/sysdolphin/baselib/robj.c to see whether this fallback behavior was refined.
Search for other HSD_ExpDesc loader functions with desc->func handling to compare NULL/default callable conventions.
Check whether any comments or documentation elsewhere explain why dummy_func is assigned when desc->func is NULL.
